bullets.text: Q1 2015 combined ratio was 92.7 with written premium growth of 8.2%; special lines contributed seasonally low loss costs and Commercial Lines produced an exceptional 83.2 combined ratio with 15% growth.
Written premium grew 8.2%; Direct auto drove strong organic growth while Commercial Lines added 15% written premium with exceptional margins, becoming a major contributor to overall results.

Agency auto remained the largest concern; a new product design was market-tested and scheduled for rollout to 16 additional states representing 40% of auto premium, plus a segmented approach to online aggregator agencies.

Direct auto posted low double-digit sales growth with both prospects and conversion rates increasing; Agency channel showed signs of improving new business flow through product design changes and ASI bundling opportunities.

Current outlook for rate need was described as modest, supporting growth momentum; Snapshot data from a large sample of users enabled modeling of gas price impacts on miles driven and frequency as an input variable.

A significant phase of a unified quoting experience platform was delivered, enabling a single quoting experience regardless of device type; this enhanced Progressive's ability to test and respond to consumer behavior.

Capital position was strong, boosted by $400 million debt issuance in January; ARX acquisition closed during Q2 2015; fully taxable equivalent total return was 1.1% for Q1, trailing prior year by 30 basis points.

ARX/ASI controlling interest was acquired, completing the cornerstone of the Destination Era property strategy; Platinum home-and-auto bundled agency product was in advanced planning for expanded rollout.
